title: WalletClient trait async_fn_in_trait suppresses dyn safety; no dyn usage possible
description: payment.rs uses #[allow(async_fn_in_trait)] on WalletClient. The comment correctly says dyn usage doesn't matter for an internal trait. But if a contributor wants to store a WalletClient as dyn WalletClient (e.g. to avoid monomorphization in a future mobile FFI layer), they will hit a compiler error about non-object-safe async methods. The current code is correct for its use case, but the allow attribute and comment don't make the constraint explicit enough for a future contributor who may try to Box<dyn WalletClient>. The maintenance consequence is a compile error with a confusing message about async methods not being object-safe, with no obvious path to fix it.
close_reason: Opinion: #[allow(async_fn_in_trait)] is correct for this internal-only trait. The trait is not object-safe by design — MockClient and LightwalletdClient are monomorphized at call sites. dyn WalletClient is not used anywhere.
